title: Refine UI buttons and add multi-payment/multi-invoice support

requirements.text: Reduce the size and simplify the styling of the Clear Records button and Download Excel button in the PaymentReportTable component to make them smaller and more compact

quotes: clear records and download button make it simple smaller

acceptanceCriteria: Both Clear Records and Download Excel buttons are visually smaller than current implementation
Button styling is simplified with minimal padding and compact text
Buttons remain touch-friendly for mobile devices

requirements.text: Change the Invoice Number field in the payment entry form to accept both text and numeric input (type="text" instead of restricted number format)

quotes: invoice number can contain both text and number format

acceptanceCriteria: Invoice Number field accepts alphanumeric input like 'INV-001', 'BILL123', or '12345'
Field validation allows mixed text and number formats
Existing numeric-only invoices continue to work

requirements.text: Add the ability to select multiple payment modes (NEFT and/or Cheque) for a single invoice number in the payment entry form, showing all relevant fields for selected modes simultaneously

quotes: add multiple payment option in single payment

acceptanceCriteria: User can select one or both payment modes (NEFT, Cheque) for a single invoice
When both modes are selected, all NEFT fields and all Cheque fields are visible simultaneously
Form validates that at least one payment mode is selected
Submit saves each selected payment mode as a separate record with the same invoice number

requirements.text: Add the ability to enter multiple invoice numbers for a single payment transaction, allowing one payment (NEFT or Cheque) to be applied across multiple bills

quotes: add single payment option for multiple bill

acceptanceCriteria: User can input multiple invoice numbers (comma-separated or multi-select field)
Single payment details (Transaction ID, Amount, Bank Name, etc.) apply to all entered invoices
Submit creates separate payment records for each invoice number with the same payment details
Form clearly indicates that one payment is being split across multiple invoices

constraints: Maintain mobile-first responsive design optimized for low-end Android phones
Keep UI lightweight with no animations
Preserve existing payment record data structure in backend
nonGoals: Payment amount validation or distribution logic across invoices
Advanced search or filtering in payment records
